title: Investment business-case thresholds
signal: gpaNRW reports that Cologne has an investment appraisal guide but had not set mandatory value thresholds under section 13 KomHVO NRW.
join_logic: Audit criteria identify the control gap; the budget provides the future investment population; the city response can establish implementation status.
hypothesis: Risk-tiered thresholds and proportionate business-case templates could focus analysis on material commitments while reducing unnecessary effort for small investments.
efficiency_effect: Less avoidable appraisal effort and fewer poorly scoped investments.
effectiveness_effect: More consistent comparison of options and public-value outcomes.
caveat: A new threshold can become bureaucracy unless proportionality and exception rules are tested.
next_test: Sample 30 planned investments across value bands and measure whether a documented option comparison exists and changes decisions.

title: Capital-plan delivery realism
signal: gpaNRW recommends budgeting investment payments only when they are likely to occur and notes Cologne has already reduced investment carryovers.
join_logic: Planned investments can be compared with actual payments and carryovers while retaining the audit's positive-control context.
hypothesis: A readiness-adjusted capital forecast could improve cash, financing, procurement, and delivery planning without cutting approved projects.
efficiency_effect: More credible resource and borrowing forecasts.
effectiveness_effect: Earlier visibility of delivery constraints and service delays.
caveat: Payment timing can move for legitimate legal, grant, market, or construction reasons.
next_test: Build a three-year plan-to-actual cohort for major investments and classify variance by readiness stage and documented cause.

title: Political visibility of credit-portfolio risk
signal: gpaNRW describes strong internal credit reporting and recommends periodic provision of its indicators to the responsible political committees.
join_logic: Debt, liquidity, participation exposure, and internal reporting can be reconciled into a decision-facing risk view.
hypothesis: A compact consolidated debt and sensitivity dashboard could improve financing decisions and reduce fragmented risk interpretation.
efficiency_effect: Faster reuse of existing treasury information.
effectiveness_effect: Better-informed financing and portfolio oversight.
caveat: Additional reporting has little value if it duplicates existing confidential or statutory reporting.
next_test: Map current reports, recipients, update cycles, sensitivities, and unresolved committee questions before designing any new view.

title: Evidence-led employee mobility program
signal: gpaNRW found no fixed responsibility or measurable goals for internal mobility management and recommended recurring employee mode-share measurement.
join_logic: Work-location and workforce context can be combined with accessibility and voluntary aggregate commuting information.
hypothesis: Location-specific measures based on aggregate mode choice and access could outperform uniform mobility benefits.
efficiency_effect: Direct mobility spending toward evidenced barriers.
effectiveness_effect: Higher uptake of feasible low-emission commuting modes.
caveat: Employee travel data can become sensitive and mode choice is constrained by disability, care, shift, and residence factors.
next_test: Run a privacy-reviewed aggregate gap analysis for three contrasting service locations using only voluntary or already governed data.

title: Municipal employee parking portfolio
signal: gpaNRW reports an outdated municipal employee-parking guideline, no overall parking concept, and potential to align charges and space use with climate-friendly mobility.
join_logic: Parking inventory, local accessibility, workforce requirements, and policy criteria reveal where spaces have different opportunity costs.
hypothesis: A site-specific, exemption-aware parking policy could improve space utilization and recover appropriate costs without impairing essential operations.
efficiency_effect: Better use of scarce municipal land and facilities.
effectiveness_effect: Align access, workforce needs, and climate objectives.
caveat: Uniform pricing or removal could harm shift workers, disabled staff, emergency readiness, or hard-to-reach sites.
next_test: Inventory spaces, permits, occupancy, costs, accessibility, exemptions, and alternative uses at five representative locations.

title: Avoided trips through service-channel and site design
signal: gpaNRW recommends considering mobility avoidance through standardized digital services and climate-friendly accessibility when choosing service locations.
join_logic: Service transactions and channel availability can be compared with small-area need and accessibility.
hypothesis: A service-by-service channel and accessibility review could reduce unnecessary visits while preserving assisted and physical access.
efficiency_effect: Fewer avoidable journeys and repeat contacts.
effectiveness_effect: More accessible service completion across user groups.
caveat: Digital substitution can exclude residents and may shift effort rather than remove it.
next_test: For five high-volume services, measure completion, repeat contact, assisted demand, travel burden, and abandonment by channel.

title: School IT software and support harmonization
signal: gpaNRW identifies incomplete school media concepts, software heterogeneity, and a substantial school-authority role in first-level support; the city describes ongoing identity-broker harmonization and legal boundaries.
join_logic: School plans, procurement, support demand, and pedagogical autonomy can be assessed together rather than as a license-only problem.
hypothesis: A supported standard catalogue with explicit exceptions could reduce support complexity while preserving lawful pedagogical choice.
efficiency_effect: Lower avoidable support and integration effort.
effectiveness_effect: More reliable and equitable digital learning support.
caveat: The school authority cannot override pedagogical autonomy or shift state responsibilities unilaterally.
next_test: Cluster the software estate and support tickets for a privacy-safe sample of schools, then identify high-volume common causes and legitimate exceptions.

title: Realized-benefit review of the citywide electronic file
signal: The city response expected the general electronic-file rollout to finish in December 2025 after user training and local implementation support.
join_logic: The planned completion milestone can now be tested against adoption, process time, storage, search, handoff, and operating-cost evidence.
hypothesis: Post-rollout measurement could identify adoption barriers and convert technical deployment into realized process benefits.
efficiency_effect: Reduce parallel paper, duplicate storage, search, and handoff effort.
effectiveness_effect: More complete, timely, and resilient case handling.
caveat: Technical login or document volume is not proof of process improvement, and monitoring must respect employee and case privacy.
next_test: Compare three pre-defined workflows before and after rollout using aggregate cycle time, handoffs, rework, storage, and user-support demand.

title: Citywide process-improvement portfolio
signal: gpaNRW and the city response describe a citywide process-management framework integrated with IT planning, including process analysis, target processes, synergies, and prioritization.
join_logic: Process demand, staff effort, service impact, IT dependencies, and budget can support transparent portfolio selection.
hypothesis: A common opportunity register could prioritize end-to-end process improvements by evidence and testability rather than organizational sponsorship alone.
efficiency_effect: Concentrate scarce improvement capacity on reusable, high-friction processes.
effectiveness_effect: Improve end-to-end completion and cross-department service outcomes.
caveat: A central portfolio can add reporting overhead and miss locally important work.
next_test: Reconstruct the current project intake and test ten proposals for baseline, volume, avoidable effort, outcome measure, reuse, and owner.

title: Risk-based IT audit capability
signal: gpaNRW describes strong IT-audit conditions but growing specialization needs; the city response cites AI, electronic files, and data analytics among future audit competencies.
join_logic: The digital project portfolio and risk landscape can be mapped against available assurance skills and audit coverage.
hypothesis: A multi-year risk-and-skills map could improve audit coverage without attempting to build every specialist capability internally.
efficiency_effect: Use scarce assurance capacity where exposure and preventability are highest.
effectiveness_effect: Earlier detection of material digital-control and compliance failures.
caveat: Audit coverage and skill gaps may be confidential; co-sourcing has independence, procurement, and knowledge-retention trade-offs.
next_test: Map anonymized audit themes and planned digital change against skills, frequency, impact, and feasible internal or external coverage.

title: Major-construction governance compliance and outcomes
signal: gpaNRW recommended binding project categorization and steering bodies; the city response states categories, bodies, cProject, and quarterly status reports are in use.
join_logic: Governance claims can be connected to capital budgets, procurement milestones, status reporting, and project outcomes.
hypothesis: Testing governance compliance against delivery outcomes could reveal which controls prevent delay and rework and which merely document them.
efficiency_effect: Reduce avoidable rework and control effort that does not change decisions.
effectiveness_effect: More reliable scope, time, and service delivery for major projects.
caveat: Complexity and market conditions confound simple comparisons between governed and delayed projects.
next_test: Sample 15 projects across categories and compare gate evidence, decision latency, scope change, procurement timing, and forecast accuracy.

title: Risk-to-continuity closure dashboard
signal: gpaNRW describes a strong risk analysis but recommends consolidated reporting, political consideration of major risks, and completion of cross-department continuity planning.
join_logic: Hazard and population context can be joined to essential services, continuity measures, owners, dependencies, and closure evidence.
hypothesis: A risk-to-service coverage map could expose unowned dependencies and prioritize low-cost continuity gaps.
efficiency_effect: Focus preparedness spending on material uncovered dependencies.
effectiveness_effect: Improve continuity of essential services during plausible disruptions.
caveat: Detailed vulnerabilities must not be published and checklist completion is not resilience.
next_test: Use controlled metadata only to map ten essential services to top hazards, minimum service, dependencies, tests, and unresolved actions.

title: Demand-based crisis on-call coverage
signal: gpaNRW recommends checking whether crisis-staff on-call arrangements match actual alerting needs.
join_logic: Historical alert demand, risk periods, response roles, staff burden, and continuity requirements can be analyzed together.
hypothesis: Risk-tiered schedules and cross-training could improve readiness per on-call hour while preserving safe redundancy.
efficiency_effect: Align paid or constrained readiness capacity with plausible demand.
effectiveness_effect: Maintain faster, more reliable activation and sustained staffing.
caveat: Rare-event readiness cannot be optimized from average historical demand, and labor rules apply.
next_test: Review de-identified alert, exercise, response-time, workload, absence, escalation, and near-miss data across several risk scenarios.

title: Youth-welfare reimbursement control
signal: gpaNRW reports that youth-welfare reimbursement cases and revenue could not be evaluated by type and recommends integrated tracking and planning.
join_logic: Entitlement, case, claim, collection, and accounting aggregates can connect service delivery to recoverable revenue.
hypothesis: A privacy-safe claim lifecycle could improve timely recovery and financial forecasting without changing eligibility or care decisions.
efficiency_effect: Recover valid third-party reimbursements with less manual reconciliation.
effectiveness_effect: Protect service resources and reduce caseworker distraction from care decisions.
caveat: Person-level youth-welfare data is highly sensitive and recovery must not delay or influence support.
next_test: Create approved aggregates for claim type, age, value band, status, deadline, recovery, write-off reason, and effort for one closed cohort.

title: Youth-welfare case-system interface
signal: gpaNRW reports paper-heavy exchange and limited transparent data flow between ASD and economic youth welfare modules.
join_logic: Process handoffs, duplicate fields, changes, delays, and system capabilities can be mapped end to end.
hypothesis: Shared master data, event notifications, and role-appropriate status visibility could reduce handoff delay and re-entry errors.
efficiency_effect: Reduce duplicate entry, paper transfer, and status chasing.
effectiveness_effect: Faster and more complete financial and service decisions.
caveat: Broader system access can violate purpose limitation; vendor and architecture constraints may dominate.
next_test: Map one high-volume handoff with fields, legal purpose, access, wait time, error modes, and existing interface capability before proposing integration.

title: District-level youth-welfare controlling
signal: gpaNRW reports that district-specific controlling across nine youth-office districts was still being built and recommends targets including expenditure, revenue, transfers, and reimbursements.
join_logic: Comparable case and finance measures can be contextualized with population need instead of treating district variation as performance alone.
hypothesis: Need-adjusted district review could distinguish structural demand from process variation and identify practices worth testing elsewhere.
efficiency_effect: Target support and process improvement where unexplained variation is material.
effectiveness_effect: Improve consistency while respecting different local needs.
caveat: Case mix, deprivation, provider market, and recording behavior can make district comparisons misleading.
next_test: Define a small aggregate cohort and test comparability of need, case mix, timeliness, cost, revenue, transition, and outcome measures.

title: Embedded youth-welfare process controls
signal: gpaNRW reports independent internal review but no standardized recurring in-process management controls in economic youth welfare.
join_logic: Error, rework, audit, complaint, and process data can locate control points that prevent harm rather than add blanket checks.
hypothesis: Risk-based embedded controls at a few critical decisions could prevent rework and financial error more efficiently than uniform retrospective checking.
efficiency_effect: Reduce preventable correction and duplicate review.
effectiveness_effect: Improve lawful, consistent, and timely decisions.
caveat: Excess controls can delay urgent support and create documentation burden.
next_test: Classify a de-identified sample of corrections and near misses by decision point, consequence, detectability, and prevention cost.

title: Public-health finance and service control
signal: gpaNRW reports that public-health specialist controlling was under construction and not yet linked to financial data; the city could not yet confirm the integration path.
join_logic: Service volumes, timeliness, quality, need, staffing, grants, and finance can reveal resource-to-service relationships.
hypothesis: A small common fact model could support resource decisions and avoid separate, non-reconcilable service and finance reports.
efficiency_effect: Improve allocation and reduce manual reconciliation.
effectiveness_effect: Direct resources toward timely, high-need statutory and preventive services.
caveat: Health outcomes are multi-causal and personal health data must not enter the opportunity register.
next_test: Select three services and reconcile aggregate demand, completed activity, wait, quality, staffing, direct cost, grant, and statutory requirement.

title: Outcome-aware public-health provider control
signal: gpaNRW recommends cross-service provider control integrated with specialist controlling; the city reports first funding notices with outcome-evidence requirements.
join_logic: Funding, target group, reach, service quality, outcome proxy, and area need can be assessed without reducing performance to spend or attendance.
hypothesis: Proportionate common outcome clauses and learning reviews could improve commissioning while limiting reporting burden on small providers.
efficiency_effect: Reduce duplicate reporting and redirect low-value measurement effort.
effectiveness_effect: Improve alignment of funded services with documented population needs.
caveat: Crude outcome incentives can encourage selection, gaming, or withdrawal from hard-to-serve groups.
next_test: Compare five funding arrangements for purpose, burden, reach, outcome logic, equity safeguards, learning use, and renewal decision.

title: Demand-based public-health workforce planning
signal: gpaNRW reports no systematic workforce-needs plan in the health office; the city response references an organizational review through March 2026 and planned recruiting measures.
join_logic: Population need, statutory workload, service demand, vacancy, skills, process change, and recruiting evidence can be forecast together.
hypothesis: Scenario-based demand and skills planning could improve recruitment priorities and reveal where process redesign substitutes for scarce capacity.
efficiency_effect: Reduce vacancy-driven disruption and poorly targeted recruiting effort.
effectiveness_effect: Sustain timely statutory and preventive health capacity.
caveat: National labor supply and future health events limit forecast accuracy.
next_test: Update the organizational review status and build three workload scenarios for five critical role families with explicit service assumptions.

title: Earlier and measurable building-permit consultation
signal: gpaNRW found consultation started after an internal note and completeness dates were not fully measurable; the city reports a two-stage note intended to start consultation earlier.
join_logic: Application milestones, completeness, consultation requests, returns, complexity, and location can separate applicant delay from internal flow time.
hypothesis: Early parallel consultation plus complete milestone capture could reduce total elapsed time and repeated case re-entry.
efficiency_effect: Reduce waiting, re-reading, and avoidable sequential work.
effectiveness_effect: More predictable lawful permit decisions.
caveat: Parallel work can create wasted review if applications are materially incomplete; complexity must be controlled.
next_test: Compare cohorts before and after the two-stage note on completeness, consultation start, response, rework, decision time, and applicant-caused delay.

title: Permit-backlog capacity allocation
signal: gpaNRW reports high unfinished permit stock, vacancies and turnover; the city cites 20 hires, reduced temporary digitization posts, case complexity, and a process-oriented indicator system.
join_logic: Backlog age and complexity can be joined to inflow, stage, skills, staffing, rework, and service consequence.
hypothesis: Cohort-based backlog segmentation and skill-aware allocation could improve throughput without treating all cases as interchangeable.
efficiency_effect: Reduce queue aging, repeated familiarization, and mismatched assignment.
effectiveness_effect: Improve predictability while preserving legal review quality.
caveat: Fast throughput is not equivalent to lawful or high-quality decisions, and prioritization has distributional effects.
next_test: Build a de-identified backlog funnel by age, stage, complexity, missing input, internal dependency, assigned capacity, and final disposition.

title: Small-area childcare capacity matching
signal: Cologne reports citywide 2025/2026 childcare coverage of 54 percent for under-threes and 101 percent for over-threes after 125 new facilities since 2015/2016.
join_logic: Citywide capacity can be disaggregated against small-area child population, forecast, travel, hours, inclusion needs, planned housing, and actual utilization.
hypothesis: A small-area capacity and accessibility model could identify local mismatch hidden by citywide target achievement.
efficiency_effect: Use existing and planned capacity where unmet demand is greatest.
effectiveness_effect: Improve practical access to suitable childcare, not just aggregate places.
caveat: Nominal places do not capture preferences, staffing closures, inclusion, hours, commuting, or provider choice.
next_test: Pilot three planning areas using aggregate demand, places, staffed availability, hours, inclusion, waiting, travel, and secured future projects.

title: Heat-action targeting and reach
signal: Cologne's 2025 heat-action reporting describes continuing measures aimed especially at older people, children, and other vulnerable groups.
join_logic: Measure location and timing can be joined to heat exposure, vulnerability, access, reach, and health-service proxies at safe aggregation.
hypothesis: A privacy-safe heat vulnerability and intervention coverage map could redirect low-cost measures toward uncovered high-risk areas.
efficiency_effect: Target limited outreach and adaptation resources more precisely.
effectiveness_effect: Increase protective reach among vulnerable populations during heat events.
caveat: Area vulnerability can stigmatize neighborhoods and cannot identify individual risk or establish health attribution.
next_test: For the 2025 season, map aggregate exposure, vulnerability, measures, channel reach, cool-place access, and heat-related service demand by safe area.

title: Cycling-intervention outcome evaluation
signal: Cologne counted 21.9 million bicycle passages at 21 permanent sites in 2024 and reported both a weather-qualified citywide decline and a seven-percent rise near one changed corridor.
join_logic: Counts can be linked to intervention dates, capital and maintenance cost, weather, construction, network context, and safety evidence.
hypothesis: A repeatable before-after and comparison-corridor method could identify which low-cost cycling measures produce sustained use and safety benefits.
efficiency_effect: Prioritize interventions with stronger observed benefit per lifecycle resource.
effectiveness_effect: Improve safe, usable cycling connectivity and uptake.
caveat: Counts are affected by weather, displacement, network changes, construction, and new counters; volume alone is not safety or accessibility.
next_test: Select five completed measures with count coverage and compare normalized volume, collisions, continuity, complaints, cost, and maintenance before and after.

title: Public-transport compensation-to-quality model
signal: Cologne publishes annual operator compensation, service, and quality information under EU Regulation 1370/2007 and maintains a long-horizon network project pipeline.
join_logic: Compensation and service outputs can be joined to quality, demand, disruption, accessibility, and upcoming capacity dependencies.
hypothesis: A stable service-and-quality bridge could make annual compensation and network priorities more decision-useful without implying simplistic route profitability.
efficiency_effect: Improve visibility of resources per comparable delivered service.
effectiveness_effect: Connect funding and infrastructure decisions to reliability, access, and capacity.
caveat: Public transport has network, social, climate, and resilience value that route-level financial metrics can obscure.
next_test: Reconcile three annual reports into stable measures and identify where compensation, service, quality, and demand cannot yet be linked.

title: Consolidated municipal-holdings exposure map
signal: gpaNRW describes high consolidated liabilities with a large share in municipal holdings and notes comparability limits; Cologne's participation reports contain entity-level three-year financial indicators.
join_logic: Core budget, loans, guarantees, entity debt, service obligations, investment plans, and cash flows can be connected while preserving accounting scopes.
hypothesis: A scope-reconciled exposure and public-service obligation map could improve capital allocation and early-warning decisions.
efficiency_effect: Reduce fragmented analysis and identify duplicated or correlated financing exposure.
effectiveness_effect: Protect continuity of important public services and informed ownership decisions.
caveat: Debt is not inefficiency; entities have different mandates, assets, accounting, regulation, and revenue models.
next_test: Create a public-data scope bridge for the six largest exposures covering ownership, debt type, city support, service mandate, investment need, and disclosure gaps.

title: Integration-service mix and outcome learning
signal: The Integration and Diversity Office reports a 2024 budget of EUR 8.7 million and reach of 20,000 people across diverse programs.
join_logic: Program resources and reach can be connected to service type, repeat use, target-group need, geography, quality, and proportionate outcome evidence.
hypothesis: A common service taxonomy and learning-oriented outcome model could improve portfolio mix without treating reach as impact or cutting hard-to-measure support.
efficiency_effect: Reveal duplication, referral friction, and reporting burden across programs.
effectiveness_effect: Improve fit between services, participant needs, and intended inclusion outcomes.
caveat: Reach may include repeat contacts, outcomes are long-term and multi-causal, and vulnerable participants require strong privacy safeguards.
next_test: Map the public program portfolio by purpose, delivery unit, target group, reported reach definition, referral path, cost category, and available outcome evidence.

title: Procurement-to-performance contract register
signal: Cologne publishes current procurement notices across recurring services, framework supplies, risk management, construction, maintenance, and assurance, but public lifecycle and realized performance are not consistently connected.
join_logic: Notice and award identifiers can anchor contract term, estimated demand, invoice actuals, operational volume, service levels, modifications, and renewal decisions.
hypothesis: A minimal contract spine could enable demand accuracy, unit-cost, service-level, modification, and renewal learning across otherwise fragmented purchases.
efficiency_effect: Improve volume estimates, contract reuse, invoice visibility, and lifecycle management.
effectiveness_effect: Connect purchased inputs to delivered service and public purpose.
caveat: Publication coverage is incomplete; supplier comparisons and live procedures require legal, confidentiality, and procurement safeguards.
next_test: After award and with approval where needed, reconstruct five closed contract lifecycles from notice through invoices, volumes, service levels, modifications, and renewal rationale.
